response:
To compute the **sample variance** of the data set:

$$
\{1, 12, -17, -3, -11, -5\}
$$

---

### Step 1: Find the **mean** of the data set

$$
\text{Mean} = \frac{1 + 12 + (-17) + (-3) + (-11) + (-5)}{6}
$$

$$
= \frac{1 + 12 - 17 - 3 - 11 - 5}{6} = \frac{-14}{6} = -\frac{7}{3}
$$

---

### Step 2: Compute the **deviations** from the mean

$$
\begin{align*}
1 - \left(-\frac{7}{3}\right) &= \frac{10}{3} \\
12 - \left(-\frac{7}{3}\right) &= \frac{43}{3} \\
-17 - \left(-\frac{7}{3}\right) &= -\frac{46}{3} \\
-3 - \left(-\frac{7}{3}\right) &= -\frac{2}{3} \\
-11 - \left(-\frac{7}{3}\right) &= -\frac{28}{3} \\
-5 - \left(-\frac{7}{3}\right) &= -\frac{12}{3} = -4
\end{align*}
$$

---

### Step 3: Square each deviation

$$
\begin{align*}
\left(\frac{10}{3}\right)^2 &= \frac{100}{9} \\
\left(\frac{43}{3}\right)^2 &= \frac{1849}{9} \\
\left(-\frac{46}{3}\right)^2 &= \frac{2116}{9} \\
\left(-\frac{2}{3}\right)^2 &= \frac{4}{9} \\
\left(-\frac{28}{3}\right)^2 &= \frac{784}{9} \\
(-4)^2 &= 16
\end{align*}
$$
